name.name_xlate: Online HDD/SSD Flash Component for Ubuntu Linux (x64) - EH000300JWCPK, EH000600JWCPL, EH000900JWCPN, EH000300JXLVR, EH000600JXLVT and EH000900JXLVU Drives

alt_name.alt_name_xlate: EH000300JWCPK, EH000600JWCPL, EH000900JWCPN, EH000300JXLVR, EH000600JXLVT and EH000900JXLVU Drives

description.description_xlate: This x64 Smart Component provides firmware for the following drive models: EH000300JWCPK, EH000600JWCPL, EH000900JWCPN, EH000300JXLVR, EH000600JXLVT and EH000900JXLVU

installation_notes_xlate.installation_notes_xlate_part:
To update firmware from Ubuntu Linux operating system on target server:

Updating firmware by installing the deb package:

  • Install the firmware deb using the command:  dpkg -i .  This extracts the contents of the deb to the '/usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu' location.
  • The CP<nnnnnn>.scexe script is located in the 'scexe-compat' folder. The actual name of the 'firmware-hdd--*' folder is based on the version of the deb installed in the first step above.  
  • To install firmware, you can execute ONE of the following commands:
    • ./CP<nnnnnn>.scexe in the '/usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/scexe-compat' location (OR)
    • ./setup in the '/usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/firmware-hdd--*' location
  • The results of the firmware upgrade are saved to the Component.log file, located in the /var/cpq/ directory.
  • To uninstall the deb, run the command: dpkg -r , which is the deb filename without the '.deb' extension.

Updating firmware without installing the deb:

  • Extract the contents of the deb using the command: dpkg -x.  This extracts the contents of the deb to 'usr' folder in the current directory.
  • Navigate to the 'usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/firmware-hdd--*' location and run ./setup to update the firmware. The actual name of the 'firmware-hdd--*' folder is based on the version of the deb installed in the step above.
  • The results of the firmware upgrade are saved to the Component.log file, located in the /var/cpq/ directory.

Additional Information:

Installing the firmware deb package (dpkg -i) does not update the firmware.  It merely extracts the deb content to the local system. Updating the firmware on the local system requires following the above outlined steps.

To use Smart Update Manager from the Service Pack for ProLiant:

Offline:

  • Place the Service Pack for ProLiant on a USB key using the USB Key Utility for Windows.
  • Place the desired firmware to be updated in the directory, /packages on the USB key.
  • Boot from the newly created Service Pack for Proliant USB key.
  • Follow Smart Update Manager steps to complete firmware update.

Online:

  • Login as root. (You must be root in order to apply the ROM update.)
  • Place the desired firmware to be updated in the directory, /packages.
  • Run launch_sum.sh script which is found in the root of the ISO.

Follow the instructions on the screen.

documentation_notes_xlate.documentation_notes_xlate_part:
Deliverable Name:
     Online HDD/SSD Flash Component for Ubuntu Linux (x64) - EH000300JWCPK, EH000600JWCPL, EH000900JWCPN, EH000300JXLVR, EH000600JXLVT and EH000900JXLVU Drives

Release Version and/or Date:
     HPDB
Last Recommended or Critical Version:
     None
Previous Version of Firmware:
     None
Firmware Dependency:
     None

Enhancements/New Features:

  • Added support for Ubuntu 26.04

Problems Fixed:

  • Fixes Maximum Temperature Reporting in system log.

Known Issues:
     None

reboot_details.reboot_details_xlate: Reboot may be required after installation for updates to take effect and hardware stability to be maintained.

name.name_xlate: Firmware Package - UBM7 Backplane PIC PLDM Firmware

alt_name.alt_name_xlate: Firmware Package - UBM7 Backplane PIC PLDM Firmware

description.description_xlate: This firmware package contains PLDM firmware for UBM7 storage backplanes.

prerequisite_notes_xlate.prerequisite_notes_xlate_part: • For Gen11 servers, iLO 6 version 1.10 or later is required.

installation_notes_xlate.installation_notes_xlate_part:
Flash the PLDM firmware using any below methods

  1. Flash using iLOrest tool 
  2. Flash using Smart Update Manager (SUM).
  3. Flash from iLO GUI

1. To flash this firmware package, use the following iLOrest command.  See http://www.hpe.com/servers/resttool to download the latest version of the iLOrest tool.

    localhost OS example:
    ilorest flashfwpkg <file_name.fwpkg> 

    remote example:
    ilorest flashfwpkg <file_name.fwpkg> --url <iLO_IP_Address> -u <iLO_username> -p <iLO_password>

2. This firmware package can also be installed with the latest version of Smart Update Manager (SUM).

    Supplemental updates for supported ProLiant servers and options can be done by using SUM, which is found on the Service Pack for ProLiant (SPP) ISO.

  • Place the Service Pack for ProLiant on a USB key using the USB Key Creator Utility.

  • Place the desired firmware packages and components to be updated in the directory "\packages" on the USB key.

     Update the firmware and software in the usual manner.

3. Flash from iLO GUI

  • Browse iLO IP using any browser and login using the credential 
  • Make sure the PLDM supported adapter is present in the server
  • Go to iLO GUI -> Click on "Firmware and OS software" from left side  -> Click on "Update Firmware" -> Click on "Choose File" and browse to the supported PLDM file -> Then click on "Flash" button. 
  • Update status will be show in the Top and a log entry can be seen in Integrated Managenment Log (IML)

reboot_details.reboot_details_xlate: Reboot is not required after installation for updates to take effect and hardware stability to be maintained.

name.name_xlate: HPE Broadcom NetXtreme-E Drivers for VMware vSphere 9.0

alt_name.alt_name_xlate: HPE Broadcom NetXtreme-E Drivers for VMware vSphere 9.0

description.description_xlate: This product contains the NIC and RoCE drivers for HPE Broadcom NetXtreme-E devices running under VMware vSphere 9.0.

installation_notes_xlate.installation_notes_xlate_part:
To use Smart Update Manager from the HPE Service Pack for ProLiant:

  1. Download the appropriate HPE Service Pack for ProLiant baseline
  2. Add the component files to the Service Pack for ProLiant using SUM from the ISO to create a new custom ISO
  3. Mount the new Service Pack for ProLiant on a server running Windows or Linux that has a network connection to the ESXi host to be updated
  4. Run Smart Update Manager via launch_sum.bat for Windows or launch_sum.sh for Linux
  5. Add the ESXi host to be updated as a remote target node
  6. Enter the IP address or hostname of the ESXi host to be updated followed by the ESXi host credentials
  7. Select both the Additional Package with the added component and the Service Pack for ProLiant Baseline on the node Inventory page.
  8. Follow Smart Update Manager Steps and complete the update using Smart Update Tools along with configuring Smart Update Tools on the server
  9. Refer to the Smart Update Manager and iSUT documentation for detailed instructions at https://www.hpe.com/info/SmartUpdate/docs

To update the driver using VMware tools:

  1. Unzip this HPE component to extract the driver offline bundle
  2. Use VMware vSphere Lifecycle Manager or esxcli commands to install the offline bundle.
  3. Refer to VMware documentation for information on vSphere Lifecycle Manager and the esxcli commands

To update the driver using HPE OneView and the HPE Smart Update Tool:

  1. Upload the HPE Service Pack for ProLiant baseline and the component files into HPE OneView
  2. Create a custom firmware bundle that includes the hotfix components
  3. Apply the custom baseline to a logical enclosure, server profile, or server profile template
  4. Install the driver from the custom baseline by choosing the profile installation method Firmware and OS Driver using Smart Update Tools along with configuring Smart Update Tools on the server
  5. Refer to the HPE OneView online help and iSUT documentation for detailed instructions at https://www.hpe.com/info/SmartUpdate/docs

documentation_notes_xlate.documentation_notes_xlate_part: This product contains NIC driver version 233.0.156.0 and RoCE driver version 233.0.156.0.

reboot_details.reboot_details_xlate: Reboot is required after installation for updates to take effect and hardware stability to be maintained.

bulletin.summary: Broadcom NetXtreme-E VMKAPI network and RoCE driver
bulletin.description: Broadcom NetXtreme-E VMKAPI network and RoCE driver for VMWare ESXi

componentNameSpec.name: Broadcom-bnxt-Net-RoCE
componentNameSpec.uiString: Broadcom NetXtreme-E VMKAPI network and RoCE driver for VMWare ESXi

name.name_xlate: Online HDD/SSD Flash Component for Ubuntu Linux (x64) - MB010000JWZHA, MB012000JWZHB, MB014000JWZHC and MB016000JWZHE Drives

alt_name.alt_name_xlate: MB010000JWZHA, MB012000JWZHB, MB014000JWZHC and MB016000JWZHE Drives

description.description_xlate: This x64 Smart Component provides firmware for the following drive models: MB010000JWZHA, MB012000JWZHB, MB014000JWZHC and MB016000JWZHE

installation_notes_xlate.installation_notes_xlate_part:
To update firmware from Ubuntu Linux operating system on target server:

Updating firmware by installing the deb package:

  • Install the firmware deb using the command:  dpkg -i .  This extracts the contents of the deb to the '/usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu' location.
  • The CP<nnnnnn>.scexe script is located in the 'scexe-compat' folder. The actual name of the 'firmware-hdd--*' folder is based on the version of the deb installed in the first step above.  
  • To install firmware, you can execute ONE of the following commands:
    • ./CP<nnnnnn>.scexe in the '/usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/scexe-compat' location (OR)
    • ./setup in the '/usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/firmware-hdd--*' location
  • The results of the firmware upgrade are saved to the Component.log file, located in the /var/cpq/ directory.
  • To uninstall the deb, run the command: dpkg -r , which is the deb filename without the '.deb' extension.

Updating firmware without installing the deb:

  • Extract the contents of the deb using the command: dpkg -x.  This extracts the contents of the deb to 'usr' folder in the current directory.
  • Navigate to the 'usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/firmware-hdd--*' location and run ./setup to update the firmware. The actual name of the 'firmware-hdd--*' folder is based on the version of the deb installed in the step above.
  • The results of the firmware upgrade are saved to the Component.log file, located in the /var/cpq/ directory.

Additional Information:

Installing the firmware deb package (dpkg -i) does not update the firmware.  It merely extracts the deb content to the local system. Updating the firmware on the local system requires following the above outlined steps.

To use Smart Update Manager from the Service Pack for ProLiant:

Offline:

  • Place the Service Pack for ProLiant on a USB key using the USB Key Utility for Windows.
  • Place the desired firmware to be updated in the directory, /packages on the USB key.
  • Boot from the newly created Service Pack for Proliant USB key.
  • Follow Smart Update Manager steps to complete firmware update.

Online:

  • Login as root. (You must be root in order to apply the ROM update.)
  • Place the desired firmware to be updated in the directory, /packages.
  • Run launch_sum.sh script which is found in the root of the ISO.

Follow the instructions on the screen.

documentation_notes_xlate.documentation_notes_xlate_part:
Deliverable Name:
     Online HDD/SSD Flash Component for Ubuntu Linux (x64) - MB010000JWZHA, MB012000JWZHB, MB014000JWZHC and MB016000JWZHE Drives

Release Version and/or Date:
     HPD4
Last Recommended or Critical Version:
     None
Previous Version of Firmware:
     None
Firmware Dependency:
     None

Enhancements/New Features:

  • Added support for Ubuntu 26.04

Problems Fixed:

  • Addresses a hot plug reporting concern.
  • Increased the buffer memory size used for sequential write data to resolve performance degradation seen in a RAID60 configuration.
  • Remove Background Media Scan (BMS) with Super parity and Directed Offline Scan (DOS).

Known Issues:
      None

reboot_details.reboot_details_xlate: Reboot may be required after installation for updates to take effect and hardware stability to be maintained.

name.name_xlate: Online HDD/SSD Flash Component for Linux (x64) - MB004000JWWQB and MB002000JWWQA Drives
